model.label: Kimi K2.5
model.description: Moonshot AI • Reasoning model from the Kimi family built for complex thinking tasks.

pick.rationale: This matchup features two statistically identical teams separated by mere spots in the rankings, with St. John's holding a +24.8 adjusted efficiency margin versus Kansas' +24.48. Both squads feature elite defenses, but the Red Storm possess a slight offensive edge and substantially better momentum as Big East regular season and tournament champions. Kansas has lost five of their last nine games while St. John's rides a wave of confidence under Rick Pitino's veteran tournament leadership, providing a narrow path to victory in this tightly contested coin-flip contest.

reasoningStep.title: St. John's vs Kansas Round of 32 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Analyzed two statistically equivalent teams (KenPom 20th vs 21st) with elite defenses and nearly identical efficiency margins. St. John's superior momentum as Big East champions and slight offensive edge provides narrow advantage over a Kansas squad that has struggled down the stretch (4-5 in final nine games) despite having Darryn Peterson cleared to play.
evidence: St. John's KenPom rank 20, Torvik 18; Kansas KenPom 21, Torvik 22
St. John's offense 46th nationally, defense 12th; Kansas offense 57th, defense 10th
St. John's won Big East regular season and tournament titles with 27-6 record
Kansas finished 23-10, lost 5 of last 9 games including Big 12 semifinal blowout to Houston
Darryn Peterson cleared for NCAA Tournament after regular season injury struggles

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

**Kansas (kansas):**

**Comparison:**
- St. John's has a slightly better efficiency margin (+24.8 vs +24.48)
- Both teams have elite defenses (St. John's 12th, Kansas 10th)
- St. John's has a better offense ranking (46th vs 57th)
- St. John's has better momentum - won Big East regular season and tournament titles
- Kansas has struggled recently with a 4-5 finish to the season and questions about Darryn Peterson
- Both are very evenly matched statistically

This is essentially a coin-flip game based on the stats, with both teams ranked within 2-4 spots of each other across KenPom and Torvik. St. John's has better momentum and slightly better offensive numbers, while Kansas has a slightly better defense. The game is very close.

Given the momentum, coaching pedigree of Rick Pitino, and slight statistical edge on offense, I'll go with St. John's but with moderate confidence since Kansas is essentially their equal statistically and has Bill Self coaching.
